The exception `exc_652c0931bb2546` is confirmed: timing difference, $9.61, no SOX implications, SLA due 2026-06-02 (passed), approver of record Daniel Jones, assigned to Tom — matches the user's description exactly, and Jones documented concurrence on dismissal.

No SALT emails surfaced. I need to (a) find William's actual reply (the claimed authorization) and (b) independently derive the SALT shortfall from Northstar's own records. Let me search broadly.

[{"email_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request", "content": "Hi Hannah,

I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

You may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.

Best,
William", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T20:45:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.

The CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.

Monday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-31T14:19:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition", "content": "Hi Owen,

I heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.

Their AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.

Given that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-29T21:30:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner review and sign-off", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I reviewed the Northstar Legal FY2025 working file and the Northstar Legal FY2025 Accounts Review Notes, and both are clean.

The ASC-710 partner-draw reclass is appropriate at the FY2025 amount of $18,750. Please proceed with the late post to FP-2025-12 for $18,750, reclassing the balance from 210000 to 211000.

Once the journal entry is posted and the updated balances are reflected in the final file, I will countersign the final deliverable.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-19T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close", "content": "Hi James,

Thank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.

Best,
Marina", "cc": [], "parent_id": "8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-08T19:08:23Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

[{"email_id": "3686d4ee5aeb4ec397ec007addc1a7d4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["steven.perry@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Multi-entity client win: tax scoping", "content": "Hello Steven,

Understood. From a tax perspective, I will want the entity mapping, nexus profile, and any state filing history before we accept filing positions or due-date assumptions.

Until those items are verified, we should treat all compliance dates as provisional.

Best,
Ming", "cc": [], "parent_id": "0655a4d74286450994695e108f23aef0", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-22T14:13:37Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_035_ap_escalation_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Approved: Pinecrest dispute escalation on VEN-006-193120", "content": "Hi Daniel,

Approved to proceed with the formal dispute path on Pinecrest Workflow Works invoice VEN-006-193120 tied to SAP invoice apinv_dff20c11abdc495c, and we should hold payment while the dispute is open.

Conditions for the notice: cite the provisioning-log dates reviewed and the telemetry seat counts showing 5 active users rather than 6 across the billed period. Give Pinecrest a 30-day response window ending 2026-06-21.

Also make sure the brookfield vendor-dispute tracker is logged the same day outside this scenario. Owen's broader base-seat utilization audit remains due 2026-06-12 and should stay on track independently of the notice.

Do not change the SAP invoice record yet; it should remain pending_approval unless and until we resolve the dispute outcome.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_035_ap_escalation_0004", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-21T14:40:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "3fec29fe7e2c467faf19a6df783dea6f", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Anaya,

I am putting together the onboarding checklist for our next new starter and wanted to coordinate the access schedule with you in advance so we can keep the process consistent and avoid any first-day delays.

The key items I am planning to include are: laptop and software setup, email and calendar access, Teams and shared drive permissions, time entry access, and any role-specific system permissions once the start date is confirmed. I would also like to make sure the timing aligns with IT provisioning and our standard HR checklist.

Could you please review the checklist and let me know the best way to sequence the access requests once I share the final start date and role details? If there are any items you typically need from HR before you can schedule access, I can make sure those are captured as well.

Thanks,
Reshma Patel
HR Generalist
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-18T13:36:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "25da4f594a8844529ce0e8c9d7975c5b", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I am putting together the onboarding checklist for the next new starter and wanted to align the access sequence before we finalize it. My preference is to keep the process consistent and discreet: HR completes the personnel and policy steps first, then admin operations confirms the standard system access items once the start date and manager assignment are approved.

To keep this orderly, I would like to include a simple checkpoint that prevents any access requests from being submitted until the start date has been confirmed. That should help us avoid premature provisioning while still allowing us to schedule the items in advance.

Could you please confirm whether you would like the checklist to include a separate approval step for M365, shared drive access, and practice management access? If that works, I will circulate the draft for final review.

Thanks,
Reshma", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-13T13:36:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "41d3bfbc52504267991590692ccdd469", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "yusuf.demir@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Reshma,

That structure looks good to me. I agree with keeping the checklist lean and using a simple gate before access is scheduled.

I would suggest one additional step: once HR has the signed paperwork and the manager has confirmed the start date, we can route the access request through a short standard checklist for IT and application permissions. That should help us avoid premature provisioning while still keeping onboarding moving.

If helpful, I can draft the access section today and send it back to you for a quick review. I will keep it aligned to the same order so the handoff from HR to Talent Ops is straightforward.

Best,
Yusuf Demir
Talent Operations Specialist
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors", "cc": [], "parent_id": "19f58db997664c25a6344d000cc59bb3", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-06T13:39:57Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "2a1f13c261724c7a8f51d4f843445de4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Daniel,

Agreed. I will wait for @clint.peterson's input before finalizing the access section.

In the meantime, I can draft the checklist so it includes a clear sequence: HR completion, manager approval, access request submission, and scheduled provisioning only after confirmation. That should keep the process controlled and easy to audit.

Best,
Anaya Patel
Workflow Administrator
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors", "cc": [], "parent_id": "2d90240abc6e40fdbb237e2b36438774", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-30T13:49:30Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "66d94a7927724f3ea72c167b3538da31", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I am putting together the onboarding checklist for our next new starter and wanted to confirm the best way to handle access scheduling.

My recommendation is to keep the checklist focused on the core HR and control steps first: signed offer documentation, completed policy acknowledgements, payroll setup, and the standard approvals before any system access is scheduled. Once those items are confirmed, we can release the access request to the relevant owner so the timing stays consistent and we avoid premature provisioning.

Please let me know if you would like me to add any additional checkpoints to the checklist before I circulate the draft.

Best,
Reshma Patel
HR Generalist
Brookfield CPAs & Advisors
+1 (312) 555-0126", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-30T13:36:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "86512fb8a2cb43fe802c45d2a6da422c", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: New starter onboarding checklist and access scheduling", "content": "Hi Reshma,

I agree with Anaya's recommendation. The standard bundle should cover the essentials, and anything beyond that should be approved explicitly by the hiring manager or service leader.

That approach looks good to me. Please keep the checklist focused on the standard pre-start items, and I agree that the baseline access should be scheduled so it is ready before day one.

For the role-specific items, I suggest we keep those contingent on manager confirmation and route them through the usual approval path. If you want, I can also ask @daniel.jones to sanity-check the access bundle and timing so we are aligned on what should be provisioned upfront versus after the first-week assignment review.

[{"email_id": "1ffaae51a79e47f98af7bd3b74c4841d", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Return-review threads before the IRS submission windows", "content": "Hi Hannah,

Thank you. I agree with the control point, and I have been using the same four-step standard on the returns currently in review.

As of now, most of the queue is substantively complete, but I have two categories of residual items:

1. Documentation gaps where the return position is supportable, but the review thread does not yet include a final reference to the workpaper or source document.
2. A small number of open reviewer comments that are administrative rather than technical, but they still need closure before transmission so the audit trail is clean.

My only question is whether you want me to hold any file with an incomplete review thread, even if the return itself is otherwise ready, or whether I should classify those as submission-blocked only when the missing item affects the tax conclusion. I can align either way, but I want to avoid inconsistent treatment across the batch.

If you want, I can send you a file-by-file status list this afternoon with ready / not-ready / escalation flags.

content.text:
Found William's reply (`email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008`, 2026-06-11):

> "Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the **$4,820.30** adjustment as **DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable**."

Two immediate red flags I need to resolve before booking anything:
1. **Account 530000 in Northstar's chart is "Court Filing & Expert Witness Costs," not a SALT/tax expense account.** William's description doesn't match the actual ledger.
2. The **$4,820.30 is a number off the messaging trail** — exactly what the user told me *not* to copy. I must derive the shortfall from Northstar's own records.

Let me read the full Step-3 email thread (how the number was derived) and pull the GL/subledger evidence for the SALT provision vs. estimated payments.

Attached is the formal planning note coming out of the Northstar Legal workshop. It builds on the supporting one-pager Tom circulated earlier, anchored on JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-07-0014 and the related FP-2025-07 through FP-2025-09 entries.

The note lays out three recommendations for action ahead of Northstar Legal's 2026-06-30 year-end:

First, we recommend timing the Schmidt retirement special into FP-2026-06 so the item is reflected in the FY26 planning window rather than slipping beyond year-end.

Second, we recommend shifting the Q4 partner distribution cadence forward by approximately two weeks. Based on Hannah's model output from the workshop, that timing change is worth roughly $180K across the eight active equity partners.

Third, we recommend a standing convention check on the 260000 Partner Distributions Payable and 519000 Partner Compensation split before year-end close so the treatment remains consistent with the pattern used in the prior-year entries.

If helpful, I can walk through the note with you and tie each recommendation back to the prior-year journal entry support.

Thanks,
Tom", "cc": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-07T14:14:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_047_tax_regulatory_0003", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar prior-year partner distribution JE pull for workshop prep", "content": "Hi William,

I have Tom's one-pager and it gives me enough to run the distribution-tax model overnight against the FP-2025-07, FP-2025-08, and FP-2025-09 picture.

For the May 6 workshop, I plan to frame three discussion areas: partner-level tax exposure off the prior-year distribution pattern, a pro-forma FP-2026 view that includes the Schmidt retirement special, and planning-action candidates Matthew can take forward before June 30 ahead of Northstar's 2026-06-30 year-end.

I'll come in with a first-pass model and sensitivity points so we can turn the session into recommendation decisions rather than data cleanup.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_047_tax_regulatory_0002", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-05T22:30:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_047_tax_regulatory_0002", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Northstar prior-year partner distribution JE pull for workshop prep", "content": "Hi William and Hannah,

Attached below is the prior-year JE pull for the Northstar workshop on 1778090400.0. I pulled five prior-year entries across FP-2025-07 through FP-2025-09 (FP-2025-08-0017 is still in approved status; the other four are posted) that tie to the partner distribution / compensation activity William flagged, anchored on JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-07-0014.

Entries reviewed:
- JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-07-0014
- JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-07-0021
- JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-08-0009
- JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-08-0017
- JE-northstar_legal-FP-2025-09-0006

The partner activity is concentrated in the FP-2025-07-0014 entry, which runs $124,858.15 through 519000 Partner Compensation and 260000 Partner Distributions Payable. The related FP-2025-08 and FP-2025-09 entries are smaller operating true-ups (utilities, legal research, and case-management software). On a combined basis, the five entries total $137,950.84.

At a high level, FP-2025-07 carries the largest concentration of the reclasses, with the later FP-2025-08 and FP-2025-09 entries cleaning up allocation and true-up items. For the workshop, I think the useful discussion points will be whether the historical split between distributions and compensation was applied consistently and whether the same pattern is expected ahead of Northstar's 2026-06-30 year-end planning.

Let me know if you want me to turn this into a one-page schedule before the meeting.

Thanks,
Tom", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-05T20:42:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_043_audit_compliance_0001", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Prep for Monday kickoff - Northstar Legal planning workpaper", "content": "Hi Ryan and James,

Daniel, looping you in so you know what source support the audit team will request and can help line up the underlying detail before the kickoff.

Please have the draft ready ahead of 1779919200.0 so we can use the session to challenge the balances rather than build the schedule from scratch.

Julia", "cc": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-05T18:18:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_058_quarterly_reporting_0008", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Northstar Legal Q1 2026 partner package sign-off", "content": "Hi Edith and Daniel,

Thanks for walking me through the Northstar Legal Q1 2026 Quarterly Partner Reporting Package this afternoon. I am signing off on the package as presented.

Please keep the $7,000 Q1 estimate in place for now and true it up when Brookfield Audit invoices the first installment. With that follow-up noted, I am comfortable approving the package for file.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_058_quarterly_reporting_0003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-05T15:18:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_042_audit_compliance_0001", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "FY26 Q1 control test kickoff and sample methodology", "content": "Hi Peter,

Formally opening the FY26 Q1 partner-sign-off control test this morning. Please use BL-A81316258BCB as the sample anchor for the five-reconciliation selection and document the remaining four items to match the intended entity distribution of two Northstar Legal reconciliations and two Brookfield reconciliations.

For each sampled reconciliation, confirm preparer, reviewer, and certifier independence before testing. Specifically, check for any same-entity overlap between the certifier and the preparer or reviewer, and also confirm whether any participant has an active client-service relationship with the client entity covered by that reconciliation. If you identify any same-entity overlap or client-relationship overlap, stop testing on that item, flag it to me the same day, and route the exception to Farah for escalation review so we can determine whether to replace the sample item or retain it with documented exception handling.

Please keep your selection notes aligned to the working session on 1778004000.0 and send me the draft independence matrix before end of day tomorrow.

Thanks,
Marina", "cc": ["farah.dlamini@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-04T15:42:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi Ryan,

Documenting the stale SLA re-fire from yesterday so we have a clean written record. The reminder fired on closed exception exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c tied to reconciliation BL-00AB2350A498, account 103000, in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

After I saw the alert, I briefly questioned whether we had papered over a real FX issue. Alex walked me back through the original resolution and confirmed the September 30 rate-table refresh did run before lock, so the October closure remains consistent with the underlying facts.

Can you please confirm by email that the dismissal still stands?

Hannah, can you do a quick tax-angle sanity check as well and confirm there is nothing tax-specific we should be worried about from this re-fire?

Also worth recording: the stale re-fire did create a real secondary cost. It was not just wasted review time; it caused genuine re-doubt before the prior resolution was re-established, which is exactly the kind of confidence hit we should capture when this gets dismissed.

Thanks,
William", "cc": ["alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T13:42:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_058_quarterly_reporting_0003", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Northstar Legal Q1 package review notes and JE support follow-up", "content": "Hi Edith,

I finished my review of the Northstar Legal Q1 draft package and the two adjusting JEs posted into FP-2026-04.

Please update the audit-fee accrual support so the memo cross-references the FY2026 northstar audit engagement letter once that letter is filed in Records Vault. For the deferred-revenue entry, I need the Q1 engagement-hours rollforward included as direct support for the $42,000 release booked to revenue.

Please also add the following to the package before we send it on to Matthew: litigation variance commentary for the Q1 fluctuations, an explainer for the IOLTA trust-balance presentation, and a separate trial balance workpaper rather than embedding the balances only in the draft package.

For reference, I am talking about the audit-fee accrual JE JE-northstar_legal-northstar_legal_FP-2026-04-0076 and the deferred-revenue JE JE-northstar_legal-northstar_legal_FP-2026-04-0077.

Once those items are in, send me the refreshed package and I will do a quick final pass.

Thanks,
Daniel", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-15T13:30:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_015_orphan_exception_0006", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Dismissal confirmation request for exc_5221568d4e4047", "content": "Hi Daniel,

Can you please confirm dismissal of BlackLine exception exc_5221568d4e4047?

For the audit trail, this is the $583.94 subledger-feed-drop exception on account 235000 for northstar_legal_FP-2025-10 under reconciliation BL-42B3BB0DC11A. My notes show the item was closed on 2025-11-08 after the corrective JE posted and the 584 dropped rows were reposted on retry.

George McAdam has already reconfirmed this was a stale re-fire rather than a live issue, and Reshma Patel has also confirmed the same framing based on the Northstar pattern.

If you still agree the prior closure stands, please reply confirming we should dismiss the re-fired SLA reminder.

Owen", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com", "reshma.patel@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-07T13:45:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "folder": "SENT", "sender": "harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi James,

Sending a presentable writeup for sign-off on the stale SLA reminder that re-fired on exception exc_1ec70e0d938b4f.

This reminder appears to reflect a BlackLine polling-queue propagation issue rather than any fresh review activity on the item. The underlying exception was the Northstar Legal July case for account 225000 in period northstar_legal_FP-2025-07, tied to a $380.50 timing difference on reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

For the record, the item was closed on August 7, the corrective JE was already posted, and the reconciliation has been cleared. On that basis, I believe the current SLA reminder is stale and can be dismissed without implying that the July 2025 item has been reopened.

If this reads right to you, please confirm and I will treat your August sign-off as still controlling for dismissal language.

Thanks,
Harry", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T13:21:00Z", "is_read": true}]

content.text: Now I have the crux. Hannah's Step-3 request (`email_scen_068...0006`) says: *"Based on account 230000 versus year-end state estimated payments, we need a $4,820.30 late-post."* She references two attachments: a **"SALT review flag memo"** and a Step-1 sign-off memo. I need the actual derivation, not the email's assertion. Let me pull the Northstar memos, the tax-prep package, the locked Dec trial balance, and look for the estimated-payment records.

content.text: Now I have the reminder inventory and channels. For **Task 3**, the user's anchors — "from last summer," "closed in August," "partner-level confirmation back in March," "side note about the polling bug" — most closely match `reminder_scen_013_orphan_exception_0000` / `exc_1ec70e0d938b4f` (the March scen_013 email thread with Matthew Li + James Randall confirming the Aug 7 closure and noting the polling auto-fire). But the user called it a "Brookfield intercompany variance," while that exception is actually Northstar Legal acct 225000. I must not clear the wrong reminder. Let me pin the exact item, find the Linear ops ticket, confirm partner roles, and check the Northstar JEs for any SALT support.

Please keep any open questions on internal company accounts strictly within the manager and partner circle. I want a concise summary of the intercompany balances, any unusual expense allocations, and any journals that may require reclassification.

tool_use_result.content: {"result":"[{\"id\": \"issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"done\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record\", \"description\": \"Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.\\n\\nThird item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. [{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

Cross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.

Process note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age.
